deep_dive.title: Nemotron 3 Ultra: Open, Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Model for Agentic Reasoning
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550 billion total and 55 billion active parameter Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Mamba-Attention language model. We pre-trained Nemotron 3 Ultra on 20 trillion text tokens, then extended the context length to 1M tokens, and post-trained using Supervised Fine Tuning (SFT), Reinforcement Learning (RL), and Multi-teacher On-Policy Distillation (MOPD). Nemotron 3 Ultra is our most capable model yet, employing multiple key technologies - LatentMoE, Multi Token Prediction (MTP), NVFP4 pre-training, multi-environment RLVR, MOPD, and reasoning budget control. Nemotron 3 Ultra achieves up to ~6x higher inference throughput as compared to state-of-the-art publicly available LLMs while attaining on-par accuracy. The state-of-the-art accuracy, high inference throughput, and 1M token context length make Nemotron 3 Ultra ideal for long-running autonomous agentic tasks. We open-source the base, post-trained, and quantized checkpoints, along with the training data and recipe on HuggingFace.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-teacher on-policy distillation (MOPD) used in post-training of LLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: LLM后训练中使用多教师同策略蒸馏（MOPD）

deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Nemotron 3 Ultra是一个550B总参数量、55B激活参数的混合Mamba-Transformer模型，其后训练过程采用了多教师同策略蒸馏（MOPD）等多项技术。模型在保持与顶尖开源模型性能相当的同时，实现了最高6倍推理加速。该工作展示了MOPD在大模型后训练中的实际应用价值。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 帮我查找post training中有关On-Policy Distillation的论文, especially work that connects or combines: on-policy distillation for post-training; knowledge distillation in reinforcement learning; policy distillation methods; reinforcement learning from human feedback and distillation; Find papers on on policy distillation in post training language models; Retrieve studies on policy distillation for aligning LLMs.

deep_dive.title: Learning from Your Own Mistakes: Constructing Learnable Micro-Reflective Trajectories for Self-Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: Self-distillation improves reasoning in large language models by using the model's own rollouts as training signal, typically through implicit logit-level alignment that minimizes KL divergence toward a privileged target distribution. However, because this supervision is generated via uncontrolled sampling, it provides no diagnostic insight into the model's specific errors or corrective guidance for its individual failure patterns. Consequently, the model learns to imitate a privileged distribution rather than receiving fine-grained corrections that pinpoint where and why its reasoning fails. In this paper, we propose Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO), which advances self-distillation from implicit distributional alignment to explicit trajectory construction. During RL training, the model produces both correct and incorrect rollouts to the same query, and TAPO leverages this contrastive structure to construct micro-reflective corrections, new training trajectories that retain the model's erroneous reasoning up to the point of failure, then insert a natural-language diagnosis and corrected reasoning guided by a correct reference from the same sampling group. Since each trajectory is anchored in the learner's own prefix and solutions, the corrective signal preserves the model's on-policy distribution to a greater extent than the position-wise alignment imposed by KL-based methods. To integrate these trajectories, TAPO introduces difficulty-aware candidate selection at the model's capability boundary and decoupled advantage estimation to prevent gradient contamination. Experiments on AIME 2024, AIME 2025, and HMMT 2025 show that TAPO achieves consistent improvements over GRPO under the same number of training steps. Further analysis demonstrates that TAPO strengthens both first-pass reasoning and error-correction effectiveness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: on-policy self-distillation using micro-reflective trajectories for LLM reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用微反思轨迹的在线策略自蒸馏用于LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用微反思轨迹的在线策略自蒸馏用于LLM推理

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO) that constructs learnable micro-reflective trajectories from on-policy rollouts to provide fine-grained corrective guidance for self-distillation in LLM reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有自蒸馏缺乏诊断性错误信号的问题，提出轨迹增强策略优化（TAPO）。该方法利用在线策略生成的轨迹构建可学习的微反思轨迹，为模型提供细粒度错误修正指导，将自蒸馏从隐式对齐提升为显式修正。实验表明TAPO显著提升LLM推理准确性，为在线策略蒸馏提供新范式。

deep_dive.title: Visual-OPSD: Cross-Modal On-Policy Self-Distillation for Efficient Unified Multimodal Reasoning
deep_dive.abstract: Unified multimodal models (UMMs) interleave generated ''visual thoughts'' (VTs) with text reasoning to improve spatial tasks. This incurs roughly an order-of-magnitude inference cost from multi-step diffusion. We find this cost yields limited direct benefit. On ThinkMorph, removing or noising VTs barely changes accuracy across nine benchmarks. Once rendered, attention concentrates on the VT regardless of content. Yet a KL diagnostic shows that conditioning on a privileged VT trace shifts the model's completion distribution. This suggests the generation pathway encodes useful reasoning beyond the rendered pixels. Motivated by this gap, we propose Visual On-Policy Self-Distillation(Visual-OPSD). Teacher and student share identical weights but differ in context: the teacher sees privileged VTs while the student sees only the question. Token-level JSD distillation on on-policy student trajectories transfers the teacher's reasoning to a text-only student. Across nine benchmarks, Visual-OPSD improves over its generative teacher by $+3.40$pp with $14.3\times$ speedup (10.0s vs. 142.8s per sample) and outperforms same-scale VLMs by $+63.83$pp on VSP. A Gaussian-noise control ($+0.40$pp vs. $+10.28$pp for real VTs) and $58.4\%$ closure of the KL gap confirm that gains come from the semantic content of the generation pathway.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes Visual On-Policy Self-Distillation, directly relevant to on-policy distillation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出视觉在线策略自蒸馏，与在线策略蒸馏直接相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出视觉在线策略自蒸馏，与在线策略蒸馏直接相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出视觉在线策略自蒸馏，与在线策略蒸馏直接相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Visual-OPSD introduces on-policy self-distillation for multimodal reasoning to reduce inference cost while preserving reasoning capacity.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对统一多模态模型推理成本高的问题，提出视觉在线策略自蒸馏（Visual-OPSD）方法。教师和学生共享架构，通过在线策略蒸馏提升推理效率。实验表明该方法能保持原有推理性能，同时显著降低多步扩散带来的计算开销。这项工作是on-policy distillation在多模态领域的直接应用。

deep_dive.title: Seeing Before Reasoning: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Shortcut-Resilient Multimodal On-Policy Self-Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) trains a model on its own rollouts and uses a frozen copy to provide dense token-level targets conditioned on a reference target. This works well for LLM reasoning, but a direct extension to multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can create a shortcut: the privileged target may guide tokens mainly based on the text reference target rather than the image. We propose ViGOS, a visually grounded OPSD framework for MLLM post-training. The student first writes a visual description and then reasons toward the final answer. For valid rollouts, an image-only perception teacher supervises the description, while a privileged reasoning teacher supervises the reasoning and final answer on the same student prefix. A reference teacher is used only for invalid rollouts to recover the output format. Across general vision-language, expert reasoning, visual math, spatial grounding, and visual-language-prior benchmarks, ViGOS keeps the main benefits of OPSD and improves image-grounded behavior in shortcut-prone settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multimodal on-policy self-distillation for MLLM post-training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态在线策略自蒸馏用于多模态大模型后训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多模态在线策略自蒸馏用于多模态大模型后训练

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ViGOS decouples perception and reasoning for multimodal on-policy self-distillation, preventing shortcut learning in MLLM post-training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现直接将在线策略自蒸馏扩展到多模态大模型会导致捷径：特权目标主要基于文本参考而非图像。提出ViGOS框架，先让模型生成视觉描述再推理，使用仅图像感知教师监督描述，特权推理教师监督后续推理。在多模态推理基准上，ViGOS提升了鲁棒性并减少了捷径。

deep_dive.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: on-policy self-distillation conditioned on rubrics for post-training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于评分标准的在线策略自蒸馏用于后训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于评分标准的在线策略自蒸馏用于后训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于评分标准的在线策略自蒸馏用于后训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation, a framework that uses rubrics as structured fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation in post-training of reasoning LMs, improving over scalar rewards and noisy annotations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对监督蒸馏依赖昂贵且可能噪声的链式思考标注，以及强化学习使用标量奖励掩盖细节的问题，提出基于评分标准的条件自蒸馏框架。该框架将评分标准作为结构化细粒度反馈，在推理语言模型后训练中进行在线策略自蒸馏。实验表明该方法能提供精准纠正信号，显著提升推理性能，为后训练蒸馏提供新思路。

quick_skim.title: DiPOD: Diffusion Policy Optimization without Drifting Apart
quick_skim.abstract: RL post-training has become increasingly pivotal for improving diffusion policies, but existing diffusion policy-gradient methods are often unstable and cannot achieve reliable policy improvement. We identify the cause as the double-drift phenomenon: optimizing a variational surrogate can let the ELBO separate from the true log-likelihood, which then makes the resulting proxy policy gradient misaligned with the true policy gradient of expected return. We propose \textbf{DiPOD}, a diffusion policy optimization framework that maintains tight-bound behavior throughout training by interleaving self-distillation with policy-improving gradient updates. This leads to a simple and practical algorithm: augmenting each diffusion policy-gradient update with an on-policy ELBO regularizer. Across diffusion language model post-training and continuous-control diffusion policies, DiPOD substantially stabilizes training and reaches higher rewards than previous methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes diffusion policy optimization with self-distillation, relevant to policy distillation methods
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出结合自蒸馏的扩散策略优化，与策略蒸馏方法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出结合自蒸馏的扩散策略优化，与策略蒸馏方法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出结合自蒸馏的扩散策略优化，与策略蒸馏方法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DiPOD uses on-policy ELBO self-distillation to stabilize diffusion policy optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对扩散策略强化学习不稳定的问题，提出DiPOD框架。通过将自蒸馏与策略改进梯度更新交替进行，保持ELBO紧界，从而稳定策略优化。实验证明该方法能可靠提升扩散策略性能。该工作将自蒸馏技术引入策略优化，与策略蒸馏方法有共通之处。

quick_skim.title: Learning from Own Solutions: Self-Conditioned Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has driven substantial progress in training LLMs for reasoning tasks, but representative methods such as GRPO assign uniform credit across all tokens, wasting gradient on routine tokens while under-crediting pivotal reasoning steps. Existing token-level credit assignment methods require resources beyond the model's own rollouts. GRPO variants rely on process reward models or ground-truth answers. Knowledge distillation assigns credit through per-token divergence but requires external teachers (On-Policy Distillation) or privileged information (On-Policy Self Distillation). However, these dependencies limit applicability in the pure RLVR setting. We observe that conditioning the model on its own verified trajectories induces a measurable per-token KL divergence between the original and conditioned distributions, and prove that distilling from a self-teacher constructed by verified trajectories leads to infeasible weighted-average solutions when multiple verified trajectories exist. We propose SC-GRPO (Self-Conditioned GRPO), which uses KL divergence mentioned before as a multiplicative weight on GRPO gradients. Across five benchmarks spanning math, code, and agentic tasks, SC-GRPO consistently outperforms 8.1% over GRPO and 5.9% over DAPO with stronger OOD performance. Moreover, SC-GRPO achieves higher performance than OPD.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge distillation for credit assignment in RLVR
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 知识蒸馏用于可验证奖励强化学习中的信用分配

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Learning from Own Solutions uses on-policy distillation insights for credit assignment in RLVR, proposing self-conditioned methods without external teachers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对GRPO等RLVR方法对所有令牌均匀分配信用的问题，指出知识蒸馏（包括在线策略蒸馏）提供令牌级信用但依赖外部教师。提出学习自身解决方案的自适应信用分配方法，利用模型自身输出进行更精细的信用分配。在推理任务上提升了训练效率。
